pr.title: fix: the custom strcpy(char *dst, char *src) and str... in...
pr.author: orbisai0security

summary: Automated security PR proposed adding a `u32 dst_size` parameter to the declarations of custom Dolphin string helpers `Strcat` and `Strcpy` in `extern/dolphin/include/charPipeline/structures/dolphinString.h`, motivated by a claimed critical buffer-overflow risk from unbounded string copies. The PR was closed without merge and had no review discussion in the provided slice. Evidence shows only header prototype changes, not implementation or call-site updates.
what_changed: Changed `u8 Strcat(char *str1, char *str2, char *dst);` to `u8 Strcat(char *str1, char *str2, char *dst, u32 dst_size);`.
Changed `void Strcpy(char *dst, char *src);` to `void Strcpy(char *dst, char *src, u32 dst_size);`.
No implementation changes, call-site migrations, matching evidence, or tests are present in the provided diff excerpt.
smart_moves: The security rationale correctly identifies that adding an explicit destination-size parameter is a common mitigation pattern for unsafe copy/concat APIs.
The proposed change keeps the existing custom Dolphin-style function names and only appends a size parameter instead of renaming the API.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation projects, changing extern SDK/header prototypes is not enough: implementations, all call sites, and ABI/matching impact must be updated and demonstrated together.

Security-motivated API changes can conflict with matching historical binaries if the original function signature is part of the target ABI; preserve original declarations unless there is project-approved evidence that the header was wrong.
Automated scanner claims should be treated as weak evidence when they do not account for decompilation constraints, binary compatibility, or source/assembly matching requirements.
A safe wrapper can be considered separately, but mutating original SDK function signatures risks breaking existing code and does not by itself fix any compiled behavior.
naming_conventions: Existing custom string helpers use PascalCase-style names: `Strcat`, `Strcpy`, `Strcmp`, `Strlen`.
Existing Dolphin typedefs are used in the header: `u8`, `s8`, and `u32`.
The proposed size argument name was `dst_size`, using snake_case for the new parameter.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were provided.
Because the diff only changes declarations, it provides no evidence that function bodies or call sites would still match the original binary.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed without being merged.
follow_up_queries: Check whether `Strcat` and `Strcpy` implementations exist elsewhere and what signatures they actually use.
Search all call sites of `Strcat` and `Strcpy` before changing any prototype.
Confirm whether `dolphinString.h` is intended to mirror an external SDK interface exactly.
If safety helpers are desired, consider adding separate bounded wrappers instead of modifying original extern declarations.
